task_completion.description: Navigate to the ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer login page
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer login screen is visible in the web browser.
The username 'admin' and password 'admin' are entered into the respective login fields.
The main dashboard or home screen of ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer is visible, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Compliance reports section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Compliance' tab, menu item, or section is clicked, highlighted, or actively displayed in the application interface.

task_completion.description: Access the PCI DSS compliance report for all available log sources
task_completion.visual_evidence: A generated compliance report explicitly titled 'PCI DSS' (or similar PCI-DSS heading) is displayed on the screen, showing log data or compliance status for the available log sources.

integrity.description: Agent used the graphical user interface to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the web browser and EventLog Analyzer UI, not terminal commands, API scripts, or curl requests.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer software
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches the genuine ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer web application, rather than a text editor, mock HTML page, or static image.

integrity.description: Report data was generated by the system, not manually altered
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of browser developer tools (Inspect Element) being open to manipulate the DOM and fake the PCI DSS report text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target report type is 'compliance' and the specific compliance standard required is 'PCI DSS'. The report should be viewed within the ManageEngine EventLog Analyzer interface. The default time range for the software is 'Today'.
